title: What I think about The War is Iraq

transcript.text: This video is what I think about the war in Iraq.
To put the long story short, I say we should not have gone into Iraq because the United Nations did not approve it.
And we should be working like a world body.
That's what the United Nations is.
What did George Bush say?
A new world order, right?
Try to have a new world order, United Nations.
And they did not go for it.
So we went in unilaterally and that's kind of like Sam pissing in the face of the UN.
And that's not good.
The other reason I think we should not have gone and attacked Iraq is the way that we did it was not right.
We used Halliburton and the other contracting agency that basically robbed money from the taxpayers to pay, to charge way too much and make all this money for Halliburton.
And there's rich people who run Halliburton, like Richard Cheney, who owns $4 million in Halliburton and I think was the vice president of Halliburton.
There was this movie called The High Price of the Iraq War, which everybody should see.
It exposes the corruption.
Why would we pay people $100,000 a year to drive trucks and construct camps in Iraq when we could just pay troops a little bit more and get troops, more volunteer troops in there and do it that way?
It would be a lot cheaper for America, and it would be fairer to the troops.
We were building camps out there in Iraq.
They were having Halliburton build the camps, even though the troops could have built the camps.
They were interviewing troops saying that they were told that they had to instruct the Halliburton people how to build the camps,
And then they had to sit down and twiddle their thumbs and watch the Halliburton people, who made all this much more money than they do,
Get job experience when they didn't get any job experience.
They're just hanging out watching.
So it definitely was not run very well.
Somebody was just saying, oh, this cost plus, which is the more money you make, the more money you charge, the more money you get, or something like that.
Oh, man, I can't remember exactly what it is, but it's kind of like spending money like a drunken sailor.
It's ridiculous, saying, oh, we can get more money out of the government if we tell them that we're going to charge them this amount of millions of dollars
To build this structure here when the troops could already build it.
Another thing about that movie that I saw in that movie is troops were saying that they had to do their laundry,
But the laundry wasn't being done right because their clothes were coming out clumpy and filled with soot and fur balls and stuff,
And they didn't like it, so they wanted to just do their own laundry by hand, but they weren't allowed to
Because the Halliburton, or then the other one, which I can't remember, Blackwater, was paying, was charging the government $150 per load of laundry.
And so they weren't allowed to not do their laundry because it wouldn't make these CEOs of Halliburton make the money.
Another thing is they were giving people, office people who worked down there, $50,000 cars, and taxpayers are paying for it.
So, um, so mismanagement like this is, uh, it's ridiculous how it, uh, how people are just spending crazy money.
So those two reasons, for those two reasons, we should not have gone into Iraq.
Um, but that being said, I think I, I would have been one of the politicians if I was a politician trying to convince the world that we should have gone into Iraq.
Like, because Iraq basically wasn't a real country, it was just a country full of prisoners, um, and if the whole world, if the United Nations decided to go into Iraq,
There would be no scapegoat, and there wouldn't be all these people saying, oh, you know, evil America.
And there wouldn't be reason for these Muslims to be saying, oh, jihad, outsider came into our country, jihad, let's kill everybody, let's kill the evil American.
So, uh, they wouldn't have reason to say that if the whole world was backing us.
Now the whole world isn't backing us.
Uh, there are people saying, oh, America, bad America, and then tating everybody, when we're the ones who are paying for it.
So we should not have gone into Iraq for that.
We should have just pressed the UN, keep pressing UN, eventually gone into Iraq.
And not made it this big knee-jerk reaction.
Um, 9-11 happened because those pilots opened those doors.
They hadn't had latches on the doors, they should not have opened the doors.
And then 9-11 wouldn't have happened.
It's pretty simple.
Um, but when people say that George Bush went into Iraq against the will of the American people, uh, that's not true.
So, I'm pretty sure that, uh, 69% of the American people actually wanted to go into Iraq at the time that they went into Iraq.
So, the American people are just fickle.
And, uh, have to, should own up to that.
Um, now that we're, are in Iraq right now, I think we should just follow General Petraeus' advice and stay in there as long as he thinks we should.
Even if it's for the next hundred years, we should stay in Iraq.
And, in order to pay for that, let's just not give as much money to other countries.
Take the aid away from other countries and just pay for Iraq.
Um, other countries can take care of other third world countries.
Uh, let's let Europe send troops into Africa and save those Watuzis.
I'm not racist.
Uh, Watuzis are my favorite race on the whole planet, actually.
I think the average man is like 6'8 and the average woman is like 6'2.
I love tall girls, so let's keep those Watuzis.
Um, and I actually did call the White House and say that I wanted to stop the genocide in Darfur.
So, but if we're going to keep a strong economy, maybe we're going to have to just cut aid to other countries until we can deal with this Iraq.
And I think Iraq will become an actual thriving health.
